name: The Oldest Warning
description: Kehl has confirmed it: the Lazarus network and the ancient Hollow in the lair are versions of the same process, separated by centuries. Kehl has asked that the Abbot hear this. Solan has the oldest records — and he may be able to trace how far back this actually goes.
accept_text: 'The Abbot,' Kehl says, and the way he says it sounds like a soldier passing a report up the chain. 'He's been watching the Wastes for forty-two years. He has the library. If the ancient Hollow in that lair have a name in the Elder records — if there's a record of when they were made, and why — Solan will find it.' He finally turns from the ridge. 'Tell him everything. What the vassal said. What I said. What Kell translated.' He looks at you directly. 'Tell him that I can feel forty-three people in this network and none of them chose to be here. And tell him that someone designed it that way deliberately, and that the design is not new.' He turns back to the view. 'The Bone Fields lists have names going back to the first great battle. Three hundred and thirty years. If something in that lair has been there longer than the war — the Abbot's records are the only place that fact would survive.'
complete_text: You tell Solan everything. The vassal's warning. Kehl's confirmation. Kell's translation of the method notation. The dead visitor in Morlak's records. The Ministry office code that doesn't exist. When you finish, Solan is quiet for a very long time. Then he stands and crosses to the library — faster than you've seen him move. He comes back with a volume from the Bone Fields series, one of the earliest, the cover worn smooth by decades of handling. He opens it near the front. 'The first great battle,' he says. 'Three hundred and thirty years ago.' He reads down a column of names. His finger stops. He reads the name aloud. It matches the marking in the lair: the personal notation, the one you brought to him in the sketch. He closes the book and does not set it down. 'They were a soldier,' he says. 'They fought in the first battle of the war. They died in it.' He looks at the courtyard. 'And then they were made into something that has been standing in that lair since before the second battle was fought.' He sits. 'The war didn't begin and then find something to do with the Hollow process. The Hollow process was here first. Someone built the war around what they were already doing.' He's quiet. 'I need you to go back to the lair. I need you to see it again, knowing what you know now. And I need you to tell me what you find.'
